message.content:
I can see you've shared a Figma prototype view, but the actual mobile portfolio app content appears to be mostly blank or very dark in the phone frame. The screen shows what looks like a dark-themed interface with some greenish gradient or background, but the specific UI elements you're asking me to analyze (text, buttons, cards, etc.) aren't clearly visible in this screenshot.

What I can see in the current view:
- A dark green/black gradient background on the phone screen
- A back arrow icon in the top-left corner of the phone screen (appears to be white/light colored)
- The phone is displayed in an iPhone 16 frame
- The Figma interface shows "Untitled" at the top with login options

For a proper analysis of the mobile portfolio design, I would need to see the actual content of the app - the portfolio items, text, buttons, cards, and other UI elements that should be displayed within the phone frame. The current view appears to be either:
1. A loading state
2. A very early draft with minimal content
3. A dark mode interface where the content isn't clearly visible

Could you please share a clearer view of the actual portfolio content, or let me know if there are specific screens or interactions I should be looking at in the Figma prototype? This would allow me to provide the detailed pixel-perfect analysis you're requesting.
